title: /kernel
summary_text: From `scripts/lib/kernel-specialist-picker.sh` (with `kernel-auth-evidence.sh`/`kernel-optional-lane-exec.sh` context): - `gemini-cli` ready means: command exists at `$(KERNEL_GEMINI_BIN:-gemini)` (`command -v` true). No auth/CLI probe is run, so ready is essentially “binary discoverable.” - Binary presence = ready. - `cursor-cli` ready means: optional `KERNEL_CURSOR_READY=true` override, or `KERNEL_CURSOR_KEYCHAIN_LOCKED_OK=true`, or cached auth evidence state `ready` for the current run, or (fallback) `command -v` on `KERNEL_CURSOR_BIN` (default `cursor`) plus `cursor agent status` output containing `Logged in as` within timeout. Notes like `workspace-trust-required`, `keychain-locked`, `status-timeout`, `status-unready` mark not-ready. - Binary presence alone is **not** enough. - `copilot-cli` ready means: optional `KERNEL_COPILOT_READY=true`, or cached auth evidence state `ready` for the current run, or `command -v` on `KERNEL_COPILOT_BIN` (default `copilot`) returns true. If the binary is absent, it falls back to `gh` auth + `gh copilot -- --help`; rc=0 there means ready. - Binary presence (or gh-based probe) is what sets ready, but if cached evidence says not-ready, that cache takes precedence. Related files clarify behavior: - [scripts/lib/kernel-auth-evidence.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-auth-evidence.sh) stores/readies states (and TTL is respected in picker via `KERNEL_AUTH_EVIDENCE_TTL_SEC`). - [scripts/lib/kernel-optional-lane-exec.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-optional-lane-exec.sh) gates execution on `kernel-specialist-picker.sh ready`.
